findings.evidence: Grep hits show the declarations at lines 43, 54, 71, and 116 with no attributes; comparable structure-map normalizations in Mathlib are tagged, e.g. `@[simp] theorem BialgHom.counitAlgHom_comp` and `@[simp] theorem BialgHom.map_comp_comulAlgHom` in `Mathlib/RingTheory/Bialgebra/Hom.lean`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to the two convolution inverse identities and to `map_antipode`; also mark `toLinearMap_comp_antipode` `@[simp]` if the linear-map form is intended as a rewrite target.
findings.issue: The new characteristic Hopf API lemmas are untagged: `antipode_convMul_id`, `id_convMul_antipode`, and especially pointwise `BialgHom.map_antipode` will not be used by `simp` to normalize antipode/morphism expressions.

summary: The public Hopf morphism API is present, but its characteristic normal-form lemmas are not registered for automation. Downstream affine-group code should not need manual rewrites to push bialgebra morphisms through antipodes or cancel the convolution inverse identities.
